applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile fusion
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to the entire ModelNew class. This auto-fuses the elementwise operations (softmax, subtract, swish, max) efficiently without manual kernel work. ConvTranspose3d and MaxPool3d remain as optimized PyTorch modules.

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton kernel with loops
attempted_but_failed.reason: Loop overhead dominated for small channel count (C=16), 3-pass kernel was 10x slower

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton kernel single-pass
attempted_but_failed.reason: Kernel launch overhead - 1.8M tiny kernels launched

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton kernel with POSITIONS_PER_PROGRAM
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton doesn't support break/return in loops

notes: torch.compile model: 2.82ms mean over 100 trials (1.10x speedup)
Key insight: torch.compile auto-fusion is more effective than manual Triton kernels for this workload
Manual Triton kernels suffer from launch overhead when processing 1.8M spatial positions
For small channel counts (C=16), PyTorch's native kernels + torch.compile fusion is optimal
The Triton kernel code is included but not used in forward() - torch.compile handles fusion better
